evals.name: event-sourcing-projections
evals.prompt:
Interview me about my Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS). We need:
- Sample tracking with barcode registration
- Experiment execution with result recording
- Event sourcing for complete audit trail (regulatory compliance ISO/IEC 17025)
- Fast read queries using projections
- PostgreSQL database
- .NET 10, EF Core

Grill me on the architecture and produce a complete Architecture-Document.md
evals.expected_output:
Architecture document that:
1. References IEntityTypeConfiguration pattern for projections
2. NO SQL DDL statements (CREATE TABLE)
3. Links to event-sourcing-projections.instructions.md
4. Shows entity class + configuration class examples
5. Uses DateTimeOffset (not DateTime)
6. Includes verification checklist in closing summary

assertions.name: no_sql_ddl
assertions.description: Architecture doc contains NO SQL DDL (CREATE TABLE, ALTER TABLE, CREATE INDEX)

assertions.name: no_datetime
assertions.description: Architecture doc uses DateTimeOffset, not DateTime

assertions.name: has_verification_checklist
assertions.description: Closing summary includes Architecture Document Verification section

evals.name: aspire-microservices
evals.prompt:
Interview me about a microservices e-commerce platform:
- 4 services: Products, Orders, Customers, Inventory
- .NET Aspire for orchestration
- PostgreSQL per service (schema isolation)
- Service discovery via Aspire
- No API gateway

Grill me and produce Architecture-Document.md
evals.expected_output:
Architecture document that:
1. References .WithReference() pattern for Aspire
2. NO hardcoded connection strings
3. Links to aspire-orchestration.instructions.md
4. Shows AppHost Program.cs pattern
5. Service discovery via Aspire native

assertions.name: no_hardcoded_connection_strings
assertions.description: Architecture doc contains NO hardcoded connection strings in examples

assertions.name: has_verification_checklist
assertions.description: Closing summary includes Architecture Document Verification section

evals.name: cqrs-without-mediatr
evals.prompt:
Interview me about a task management system:
- CQRS without MediatR (direct handler injection)
- Commands: CreateTask, UpdateTask, CompleteTask
- Queries: GetTaskById, ListTasks with filters
- ASP.NET Core Minimal API
- Clean Architecture (Domain, Application, Infrastructure, Services)

Grill me and produce Architecture-Document.md
evals.expected_output:
Architecture document that:
1. Shows direct ICommandHandler injection (NO MediatR)
2. Individual command parameters (NOT model objects)
3. Links to cqrs-implementation.instructions.md
4. Shows Minimal API endpoint pattern with direct injection
5. Result<T> pattern for error handling

assertions.name: no_mediatr_references
assertions.description: Architecture doc contains NO MediatR references

assertions.name: has_direct_handler_injection
assertions.description: Architecture doc shows ICommandHandler<TCommand, Result<TResponse>> pattern

assertions.name: has_individual_parameters
assertions.description: Architecture doc shows individual parameters (not model objects) in commands
assertions.description_extended: Command constructors should have individual parameters like (string code, string title, Guid protocolId), NOT (CreateProductModel model)

assertions.name: has_verification_checklist
assertions.description: Closing summary includes Architecture Document Verification section
